summary: A Ukrainian drone attack struck the Russian frigate "Admiral Essen" in Novorossiysk Bay, damaging its middle superstructure and detonating PK-10 passive barrier grenade launchers, along with the TK-25 electronic warfare complex. Ukrainian operators conducted FPV strikes over 50 km into occupied Donetsk, hitting military and logistics vehicles. The enemy has begun deploying fixed-wing UAVs carrying two FPV drones each, prompting calls for enhanced anti-drone defenses including frequency detectors, shotguns, and AK rifle cartridges with anti-drone capsules. Ukrainian firm Vyriy is developing new "Lightning" UAV variants with alternative control systems and retargeting capabilities. Russian forces, including the "Rubicon" Center, reported intercepting over 1,500 fixed-wing UAVs and downing more than 70 enemy drone models, with 75% of intercepts involving just 11 Ukrainian drone types. Russian operators also used ZALA Lancet loitering munitions to strike group targets amid active electronic warfare.
